rules.name: readme-before-edit
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., `packages/*`, `crates/*`), read all `README.md` files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.name: readme-before-create
rules.evidence: Before editing or creating files in any subdirectory (e.g., `packages/*`, `crates/*`), read all `README.md` files in the directory path from the repo root up to and including the target file's directory.

rules.name: start-dev-server-before-source-edit
rules.evidence: If you are changing Next.js source or integration tests, start `pnpm --filter=next dev` in a separate terminal session before making edits (unless it is already running). If you skip this, explicitly state why (for example: docs-only, read-only investigation, or CI-only analysis).

rules.name: no-check-deprecated-edit
rules.evidence: Do NOT use `check()` - it is deprecated. Use `retry()` + `expect()` instead

rules.name: no-check-deprecated-write
rules.evidence: Do NOT use `check()` - it is deprecated. Use `retry()` + `expect()` instead

rules.name: use-retry-not-settimeout-edit
rules.evidence: Use `retry()` from `next-test-utils` instead of `setTimeout` for waiting ... // Bad - don't use setTimeout for waiting

rules.name: use-retry-not-settimeout-write
rules.evidence: Use `retry()` from `next-test-utils` instead of `setTimeout` for waiting ... // Bad - don't use setTimeout for waiting

rules.name: prefer-fixture-dirs-edit
rules.evidence: Prefer real fixture directories over inline `files` objects

rules.name: prefer-fixture-dirs-write
rules.evidence: Prefer real fixture directories over inline `files` objects

rules.name: use-new-test-for-generation
rules.evidence: Generating tests using `pnpm new-test` is mandatory.

rules.name: no-pr-ready
rules.evidence: Do NOT mark PRs as "ready for review" (`gh pr ready`) - leave PRs in draft mode and let the user decide when to mark them ready

rules.name: no-claude-code-footer
rules.evidence: Do NOT add "Generated with Claude Code" or co-author footers to commits or PRs

rules.name: include-next-js-llm-marker
rules.evidence: When writing full PR/Issue/Discussion descriptions, commit messages, or leaving comments on pull requests, you MUST include the following HTML comment at the bottom of the description or comment: <!-- NEXT_JS_LLM -->

rules.name: rebuild-after-branch-switch
rules.evidence: Always run a full bootstrap build after switching branches

rules.name: prevalidate-before-commit
rules.evidence: Pre-validate before committing to avoid slow lint-staged failures (~2 min each)

rules.name: rebuild-before-integration-tests
rules.evidence: When running Next.js integration tests, you must rebuild if source files have changed

rules.name: no-remote-url-mutation
rules.evidence: Do not switch remotes to HTTPS, mutate remote URLs, retry repeatedly, or attempt another authentication workaround unless the user explicitly requests it.

rules.name: ssh-preflight-before-force-push-rebase
rules.evidence: Before a force-push or stack rebase that may hydrate partial-clone objects, prefer a lightweight SSH preflight.

rules.name: avoid-super-imports-rust-edit
rules.evidence: Avoid adding new `super::` imports except in inline `mod` blocks (e.g. `mod tests { ... }`) — prefer `crate::`-rooted paths.

rules.name: avoid-super-imports-rust-write
rules.evidence: Avoid adding new `super::` imports except in inline `mod` blocks (e.g. `mod tests { ... }`) — prefer `crate::`-rooted paths.

rules.name: no-browser-back-router-act-tests-edit
rules.evidence: Never use `browser.back()` to return to a page where accordion links are already visible — BFCache restores state and triggers uncontrolled re-prefetches.

rules.name: no-browser-back-router-act-tests-write
rules.evidence: Never use `browser.back()` to return to a page where accordion links are already visible — BFCache restores state and triggers uncontrolled re-prefetches.

rules.name: count-highlight-line-numbers-edit
rules.evidence: When adding `highlight={...}` attributes to code blocks, carefully count the actual line numbers within the code block

rules.name: count-highlight-line-numbers-write
rules.evidence: When adding `highlight={...}` attributes to code blocks, carefully count the actual line numbers within the code block

skill_candidates.summary: Fast local iterative dev loop: start watch build in background, run the mode-matching focused test script, kill the watch process when done
skill_candidates.evidence: **1. Start watch build in background:** ... **2. Run focused tests with the matching mode script:** ... **3. When done, kill the background watch process (if you started it).**

skill_candidates.summary: Capture test/build output to a file once, then re-analyze from the saved file with grep/tail instead of re-running commands
skill_candidates.evidence: Never re-run the same test suite with different grep filters. Capture output once to a file, then read from it: ... grep "●" /tmp/test-output.log ... grep -A5 "Error:" /tmp/test-output.log ... tail -5 /tmp/test-output.log

always_on: Monorepo/codebase structure overview and package directory map (informational, not action-bound)
Key entry points and key-directories quick reference (informational)
Bundler selection facts: Turbopack is the default for dev/build; there is no --no-turbopack flag
Fork-PR vs branch-PR permission judgment before writing PR descriptions (needs a runtime org-membership check, not regex-triggerable)
GitHub Issues/Discussions permission judgment for external contributors (same membership-check caveat)
Secrets and env safety principles: never print/paste secret values, ask the user rather than invent placeholder credentials, redact sensitive output when sharing
GitHub SSH authentication failure handling: stop and ask the user (reactive to error text seen after a command runs, not a pre-call trigger)
CI/PR-status triage prioritization judgment calls (blocking failures first, treat failures as real, reproduce with matching CI env) — largely already routed to the existing $pr-status-triage skill
Development Tips env var facts (DEBUG=next:*, NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1)
Runtime/bundling domain rules for flag wiring, DCE require() patterns, edge builds, and entry-base.ts import boundaries — need domain judgment, not reliably regex-detectable
Test Gotchas collection (PPR/cache-components behavior, mode-specific skipStart pattern, snapshot env-flag sensitivity, app-page.ts build-template require() constraint, CI env reproduction tips)
Rust/Cargo fmt ordering convention and ICE troubleshooting tip (delete incremental artifacts and retry)
Server security header-filtering review policy (flag non-standard header reads) — requires judging "standard vs non-standard" header, not regex-safe
Granular context-efficiency tips beyond the two skill candidates (batching edits before one build, caching external API responses to a variable/file)
